root_text.sn30.4-6:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn30.4-6:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā jalābujānaṁ supaṇṇānaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn30.4-6:1.6: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekacco kāyena dvayakārī hoti, vācāya dvayakārī, manasā dvayakārī.
root_text.sn30.4-6:1.10: ‘aho vatāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ supaṇṇānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn30.4-6:1.11: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ supaṇṇānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn30.4-6:1.12: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ supaṇṇānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the womb-born phoenixes …”
translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.6: “Mendicant, it’s when someone does both kinds of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.8: ‘The spontaneously-born phoenixes are long-lived, beautiful, and very happy.’
translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.10: ‘If only, when my body breaks up, after death, I would be reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born phoenixes!’
translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.11: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born phoenixes.
translation_text.sn30.4-6:1.12: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born phoenixes.”
